teamObjective.explanation: Player 1 is down to 2 cards, so the immediate priority is to make that opponent spend cards rather than giving them a cheap path to finish. Leading a low single forces the table to respond and can burn one of their remaining cards.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) hand is unknown. They have 3 cards and may be able to cover a low lead or win a later trick, but no public signals have been exchanged.
evidence: publicHistory only contains new_game; no partner signals observed

opponentBelief.summary: Player 1 is an opponent near finish with only 2 cards remaining. Those cards are unknown but likely include at least one playable answer to a low single lead.

actionRationale.primaryReason: My hand is four consecutive low clubs (3-6), all non-trump. With no trump, bomb, or straight available, the standard lead is the lowest single. Playing the 3♣ gets rid of the weakest card first, preserves the slightly higher 4♣-6♣ for later tricks, and forces opponents to commit a card to the trick.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 4♣ is not materially stronger than the 3♣ in absolute terms; leading it wastes a small amount of ordering value and still loses to almost any non-trump or trump card.
The 5♣ is the middle of my hand and my third-weakest card; leading it now leaves me with weaker cards for later while still being unlikely to win the trick.
The 6♣ is my highest non-trump card. Leading it squanders the only card that might occasionally win against another low club lead later, and it is still very weak against trumps or higher ranks.

risks: A near-finish opponent may beat the low lead and then use their remaining card to go out on the next trick.
Partner may be unable to cover, ceding lead and tempo to the opponents.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Because all my cards are low non-trump singles, no lead can strongly control the table. By leading the 3♣ I at least minimize my own loss of value and ensure the opponent must play a real card rather than passing a weak lead uncontested.

notes: No trump, bomb, or multi-card combination is available in this hand, so the decision reduces to which singleton to lead.
